2605.18646.title: Language-Switching Triggers Take a Latent Detour Through Language Models
2605.18646.paper_summary: Backdoor attacks on language models pose a growing security concern, yet the internal mechanisms by which a trigger sequence hijacks model computations remain poorly understood. We identify a circuit underlying a language-switching backdoor in an 8B-parameter autoregressive language model, where a three-word Latin trigger (nine tokens) redirects English output to French. We decompose the circuit into three phases: (1) distributed attention heads at early layers compose the trigger tokens into the last sequence position; (2) the resulting signal propagates through mid-layers in a subspace orthogonal to the model's natural language-identity direction; (3) the MLP at the final layer converts this latent signal into French logits. The entire circuit flows through a serial bottleneck at a single position: corrupting that position at any layer entirely mitigate the trigger but also hinder the model's capabilities. The orthogonal latent encoding suggests that defenses that search for language-like signals in intermediate representations would miss this trigger entirely.
2605.18646.paper_summary_zh: 對語言模型的後門攻擊日益成為安全隱患，但觸發序列劫持模型計算的內部機制仍然不甚了解。我們識別出一個基於語言切換的後門電路，該電路存在於一個具有8B參數的自回歸語言模型中，其中一個三字拉丁觸發詞（九個標記）將英語輸出重定向至法語。我們將該電路分解為三個階段：（1）早期層的分佈式注意力頭將觸發標記組合到最後的序列位置；（2）產生的信號通過中層在與模型的自然語言身份方向正交的子空間中傳播；（3）最後一層的多層感知器將這一潛在信號轉換為法語邏輯值。整個電路在單一位置流經一個串行瓶頸：在任何層中損壞該位置將完全消除觸發，但也會妨礙模型的能力。正交潛在編碼表明，尋找中間表示中類似語言信號的防禦措施將完全錯過這一觸發。
2605.18646.author: Francis Kulumba et.al.
2605.18646.authors: Francis Kulumba, Wissam Antoun, Théo Lasnier, Benoît Sagot, Djamé Seddah
